metadata.purpose: Optional exact-course overlays. Generic surface/distance/style weights remain the default; these presets only correct known track-timing effects documented in CM9-CM16 guides.

cm16_nakayama_1200_turf.label: CM16 — Nakayama Turf 1200 m
cm16_nakayama_1200_turf.notes: Position before the acceleration zone is unusually important. Nimble Navigator is intentionally left unchanged outside Front: some over-weighting is acceptable because the skill remains strong.

rules.reason: Guide emphasizes corner velocity for entering the acceleration phase in position.

cm15_hanshin_2200_turf.label: CM15 — Hanshin Turf 2200 m
cm15_hanshin_2200_turf.notes: Groundwork, Dodging Danger, Prudent Positioning and Uma Stan are handled by the global corrections; no additional track-only rule is required.

cm14_tokyo_1600_turf.label: CM14 — Tokyo Turf 1600 m
cm14_tokyo_1600_turf.notes: Final Push has useful track timing despite a generic false zero. Nimble remains generic because a moderate over-weight is acceptable.

rules.reason: Final-corner timing is usable on Tokyo 1600 for Front Runner.

cm13_tokyo_2400_turf.label: CM13 — Tokyo Turf 2400 m
cm13_tokyo_2400_turf.notes: The multiple uphill sections make 1,500,000 CC materially more reliable for Late Surger than on a generic Medium course.

rules.reason: Tokyo 2400 provides several favorable uphill activation windows.

cm12_nakayama_2000_turf.label: CM12 — Nakayama Turf 2000 m
cm12_nakayama_2000_turf.notes: Generic Medium weights and the global Nimble correction are adequate for this prototype.

cm11_hanshin_3200_turf.label: CM11 — Hanshin Turf 3200 m
cm11_hanshin_3200_turf.notes: The generic batch uses a shorter Long representative course and underestimates Nimble's available late-race blocking window.

rules.reason: Long 3200 m race gives more late-race traffic opportunities.
Long 3200 m race gives more late-race traffic opportunities.
Long 3200 m race gives more late-race traffic opportunities.

cm10_tokyo_1600_dirt.label: CM10 — Tokyo Dirt 1600 m
cm10_tokyo_1600_dirt.notes: The uphill overlaps a useful acceleration window, making Highlander much better than its generic representative-course score.

rules.reason: Tokyo Dirt 1600 uphill timing makes the skill a viable acceleration option.

cm9_chukyo_1200_turf.label: CM9 — Chukyo Turf 1200 m
cm9_chukyo_1200_turf.notes: Chukyo's uphill timing makes Highlander valuable; corners also receive an extra course-specific positioning bonus.

rules.reason: The only uphill lies in a favorable acceleration window.
Guide emphasizes corner velocity and carryover into the decisive section.
